items.title: IBM’s Market Cap Collapse
items.description: IBM (NYSE: IBM) ranked No. 1 by market capitalization in the US in 1985, according to a study by Axios. By 1995 and 2005, under legendary CEO Jack Welch (who retired in 2001), GE was the world’s largest conglomerate and ranked in first place. GE was surpassed by Apple (NASDAQ: AAPL) in 2015 as the ... items.title: A Look At IBM (IBM) Valuation As Short Seller Critique Sparks Fresh Doubts On Its AI Direction
items.description: International Business Machines (IBM) stock has come under pressure after high profile short seller Jim Chanos questioned the company’s artificial intelligence focus, criticizing an emphasis on marketing and consulting instead of core technology development. See our latest analysis for International Business Machines. The recent criticism has landed during a weak patch for the stock, with a 7 day share price return of an 11.46% decline and a 30 day share price return of a 15.49% decline...
